name: Greek Chicken Salad
description: With Feta, Olives & Lemon Dressing
steps: Slice the chicken breast into strips and season with black pepper and dried oregano.
Heat one tablespoon of olive oil in a pan and cook the chicken until golden and cooked through.
Whisk the lemon juice and zest with the remaining olive oil to make a simple dressing.
Drain the cannellini beans and arrange them with the baby spinach, cherry tomatoes, cucumber and red onion in bowls.
Add the warm chicken on top of the salad.
Scatter over the feta and black olives.
Drizzle with the lemon dressing and toss lightly.
Serve straight away while the chicken is still warm.
tip: Keep the chicken in large strips so it stays juicy and slices neatly through the salad.

name: BBQ Salmon with Spanish Grains
description: With Tenderstem Broccoli, Mange Tout & Lemon
steps: Cook the wholegrain Spanish grains according to the packet instructions and keep them warm.
Toss the tenderstem broccoli and mange tout with extra virgin olive oil, Easy Garlic, smoked paprika, salt and pepper.
Spread the vegetables over a lined baking tray and roast until just tender and lightly charred.
Season the salmon with black pepper and place it on the tray beside the vegetables.
Roast until the salmon flakes easily and the vegetables are browned at the edges.
Squeeze over fresh lemon juice and sprinkle with chopped dill.
Fluff the grains and fold through the lemon zest.
Serve the salmon over the Spanish grains with the vegetables alongside.
tip: Preheat the baking tray with the oven so the vegetables start to char as soon as they go in.

name: Chicken Pitta with Tzatziki
description: With Salad, Hummus, Tomatoes & Red Onion
steps: Season the chicken with black pepper, oregano and smoked paprika, then cook it in a hot pan until browned and fully cooked.
Warm the wholemeal pittas so they are soft and ready to fill.
Slice the tomatoes and red onion and toss them with the baby spinach.
Stir a little lemon juice into the tzatziki for extra freshness.
Split the pittas and spread each one with hummus.
Fill with salad, chicken, feta and red onion.
Spoon in the tzatziki and finish with a squeeze of lemon.
Serve immediately while the pittas are warm.
tip: Warm the pittas just before serving so they do not split when filled.

name: BBQ Chicken Souvlaki
description: With Tzatziki, Greek Salad & Warm Pitta
steps: Cut the chicken into strips and marinate with extra virgin olive oil, Easy Garlic, oregano, lemon juice and zest, and black pepper.
Cook the chicken on a hot griddle, lined baking tray or air-fryer basket until nicely charred and cooked through.
Prepare a Greek salad with cucumber, cherry tomatoes, red onion, feta and olives.
Warm the pittas in a dry pan or oven.
Stir the tzatziki so it is smooth and ready to spoon over the chicken.
Pile the salad into each pitta and top with the chicken strips.
Add a generous spoonful of tzatziki and a little extra lemon if needed.
Serve with the remaining salad on the side.
tip: If time allows, marinate the chicken for 30 minutes before cooking for extra flavour.

name: Mediterranean Chicken Wrap
description: With Hummus, Feta & Baby Spinach
steps: Season the chicken with black pepper, oregano and smoked paprika, then cook until golden and cooked through.
Warm the wraps so they are easier to fold.
Mix the baby spinach with cucumber, cherry tomatoes and red onion.
Spread each wrap with hummus.
Add the chicken on top of the hummus layer.
Pile in the salad and crumble over the feta.
Finish with lemon juice; the hummus will keep the wrap moist without extra oil.
Roll up tightly and serve.
tip: Keep the filling just off-centre so the wrap rolls more neatly.

name: Turkey & Bean Chilli
description: With Rice & Fresh Coriander
steps: Cook the basmati rice and keep it covered so it stays fluffy.
Heat the olive oil in a pan and soften the onion and red pepper.
Add the garlic and turkey mince, breaking the meat up as it browns.
Stir in smoked paprika, cumin and chilli flakes.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes and simmer for a few minutes.
Add the red kidney beans and cook until the chilli thickens.
Season to taste and stir through most of the fresh coriander.
Serve the chilli over the rice with the remaining coriander on top.
tip: Let the chilli simmer uncovered at the end so the sauce reduces cleanly.

name: Tuna & Cannellini Bean Salad
description: With Cherry Tomatoes, Rocket & Lemon Dressing
steps: Season the tuna steaks and sear them quickly in a hot pan.
Drain and rinse the cannellini beans.
Whisk together lemon juice, lemon zest, olive oil, capers and black pepper.
Slice the cherry tomatoes, cucumber and red onion.
Toss the rocket with the beans and vegetables in a large bowl.
Slice the tuna and lay it over the salad.
Scatter over the chopped parsley and drizzle with the lemon dressing.
Serve while the tuna is still warm.
tip: Do not overcook the tuna; it should stay tender in the centre.

name: Herb & Lemon Mackerel with Spanish Grains
description: With Tenderstem Broccoli, Mange Tout & Lemon
steps: Heat the oven and cook the wholegrain Spanish grains according to the packet instructions.
Mix the extra virgin olive oil with the chopped dill, crushed garlic, lemon zest, lemon juice, salt and black pepper.
Place each mackerel fillet on its own sheet of foil and spoon over the herb and lemon blend.
Seal each foil parcel loosely, place on a baking tray and bake until the mackerel flakes easily.
Steam or quickly pan-cook the tenderstem broccoli and mange tout so they remain bright and crisp.
Open the foil parcels carefully and spoon the warm herb and lemon juices back over the fish.
Fluff the Spanish grains and check the seasoning of the vegetables.
Serve each mackerel parcel with the Spanish grains and vegetables.
tip: Leave a little air inside each foil parcel so the herb and lemon steam can circulate around the mackerel.

name: Chicken Shakshuka
description: With Spinach, Feta & Wholemeal Pitta
steps: Cook the chicken in a large pan until lightly browned and almost cooked through.
Add extra virgin olive oil, onion and red pepper and soften them for a few minutes.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, smoked paprika, cumin and chilli flakes.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes and simmer until the sauce thickens slightly.
Stir in the baby spinach until wilted, then make two small wells in the sauce and crack in the eggs.
Cover the pan and cook until the egg whites are set but the yolks are still soft.
Scatter the feta over the top and warm the pittas separately.
Serve the shakshuka with the pittas for scooping.
tip: Keep the heat fairly low once the eggs go in so the yolks stay soft.

name: Pesto Basa Pasta
description: With Cherry Tomatoes & Parmesan
steps: Cook the wholemeal penne until al dente, reserving a little pasta water before draining.
Heat the extra virgin olive oil in a pan and soften the onion and Easy Garlic.
Add the basa fillets and cook gently until they start to flake.
Stir in the green pesto and cherry tomatoes.
Add the baby spinach and let it wilt into the sauce.
Drain the pasta and toss it through the fish and pesto mixture.
Finish with lemon juice, chopped basil and grated parmesan.
Serve immediately while the sauce is glossy and warm.
tip: Fold the fish into the pasta gently so the basa stays in large flakes.

name: Thai Fishcakes
description: With Sweet Chilli Dip & Asian Slaw
steps: Flake the pollock and mix it with the egg, panko, spring onions and coriander.
Season the mixture with salt, pepper and a squeeze of lime.
Shape into small fishcakes and chill briefly if the mixture feels soft.
Mix the Greek yoghurt, honey, chilli flakes and a little lime juice for the dip.
Toss the bagged rainbow slaw with sesame oil, low-salt soy sauce and lime.
Place the fishcakes on a lined baking tray, brush lightly with extra virgin olive oil and bake until crisp, golden and cooked through.
Serve the fishcakes with the sweet chilli dip and the slaw on the side.
Finish with extra coriander if you want a fresher flavour.
tip: Chilling the fishcakes for a few minutes helps them hold together while baking.

name: Herb Crusted Cod
description: With Garlic Roasted Vegetables
steps: Preheat the oven and chop the courgette, aubergine and red pepper into even pieces.
Toss the vegetables with extra virgin olive oil, Easy Garlic, salt and pepper.
Roast the vegetables until tender and lightly browned.
Mix the panko with chopped parsley, dried thyme and lemon zest.
Brush the cod with Dijon mustard and press the herb crumb onto the top.
Bake the cod until it flakes easily and the topping is golden.
Squeeze lemon over the fish and vegetables.
Serve the cod with the garlic roasted vegetables.
tip: Buying pre-cut Mediterranean vegetables makes this a quick one-tray dinner; otherwise cut everything to a similar size so it roasts evenly.

name: Mediterranean Baked Salmon
description: With Roasted Vegetables & Wholewheat Couscous
steps: Prepare the wholewheat couscous in a separate bowl and fluff it with a fork once tender.
Chop the courgette, aubergine, pepper, cherry tomatoes and red onion into roasting pieces.
Toss the vegetables with extra virgin olive oil, oregano, salt and pepper.
Roast the vegetables until softened and lightly caramelised.
Season the salmon and place it on top of the tray for the final part of cooking.
Bake until the salmon is just cooked through.
Finish with lemon juice and chopped basil and parsley.
Serve the salmon with the vegetables and couscous.
tip: Add the salmon part-way through roasting so it stays tender and does not dry out.

name: Halloumi & Roasted Vegetable Couscous
description: With Lemon & Fresh Herbs
steps: Cook the wholewheat couscous, then fluff it with a fork and keep it warm.
Chop the vegetables into bite-sized pieces and toss with extra virgin olive oil, smoked paprika and black pepper.
Roast the vegetables until soft and slightly browned.
Slice the halloumi and fry it until golden on both sides.
Drain the chickpeas and mix them into the couscous with the roasted vegetables.
Stir through lemon juice and chopped parsley and mint.
Top with the halloumi slices.
Serve warm with an extra squeeze of lemon.
tip: Pat the halloumi dry and use a separate very hot pan to give it the best golden crust.

name: Lemon Garlic Basa & Prawn Rice
description: With Cherry Tomatoes, Spinach & Fresh Parsley
steps: Cook the wholegrain brown rice according to the packet instructions and keep it covered.
Pat the basa dry, season with black pepper and cook it gently in a large frying pan until it begins to flake.
Move the basa to the edge of the pan, add the extra virgin olive oil and soften the Easy Garlic with the chilli flakes.
Add the cherry tomatoes and cook until they soften and begin to burst.
Stir in the prawns and cook until pink, opaque and cooked through, keeping the basa in large flakes.
Add the baby spinach and let it wilt into the tomato and prawn mixture.
Fold in the cooked brown rice, lemon juice and chopped parsley, then season with the measured salt and black pepper.
Serve immediately with an extra squeeze of lemon if desired.
tip: Microwave wholegrain rice makes this a one-pan dinner; fold gently so the rice and basa retain their texture.

name: Chicken Shawarma Bowls
description: With Wholewheat Couscous, Spinach & Garlic Yoghurt
steps: Mix the chicken with extra virgin olive oil, cumin, smoked paprika, ground coriander, sumac and one teaspoon of Easy Garlic.
Cook the chicken in a hot pan until browned and cooked through.
Prepare the wholewheat couscous according to the packet instructions.
Chop the baby spinach, cucumber, cherry tomatoes and red onion.
Stir lemon juice and the remaining Easy Garlic into the Greek yoghurt.
Fluff the couscous and divide it between bowls.
Add the salad and top with the chicken.
Spoon over the garlic yoghurt and serve.
tip: Toast the spices briefly with the chicken; keep the sumac measured to a quarter teaspoon so its sharp flavour stays balanced.

name: Healthy Chicken Gyros
description: With Homemade Tzatziki, Salad & Warm Pitta
steps: Season the chicken with extra virgin olive oil, oregano, smoked paprika and black pepper.
Cook the chicken in a hot pan until it is browned and cooked through.
Mix the baby spinach, cucumber, cherry tomatoes and red onion in a bowl.
Warm the pittas.
Stir a little lemon juice into the tzatziki for a brighter flavour.
Fill each pitta with the salad and chicken.
Add the feta and spoon over the tzatziki.
Serve immediately while the pittas are still warm.
tip: Batch-cook the seasoned chicken for this and the other pitta recipes, then slice it thinly after reheating for quick assembly.

name: Turkey Meatballs in Tomato Sauce
description: With Wholemeal Pasta & Parmesan
steps: Mix the turkey mince with the egg, breadcrumbs, parmesan, salt and pepper.
Shape the mixture into meatballs.
Brown the meatballs in olive oil in a large pan.
Add the fresh onion and Easy Garlic and soften them gently.
Pour in the passata and simmer until the sauce thickens.
Cook the wholemeal penne until al dente.
Wilt the baby spinach into the sauce, then toss through the wholemeal penne, meatballs and basil.
Serve with extra parmesan scattered on top.
tip: Wet your hands slightly when rolling the meatballs to stop the mixture sticking.

name: Chicken Baked Feta Pasta
description: With Roasted Cherry Tomatoes & Fresh Basil
steps: Heat the oven and cut the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces.
Place the cherry tomatoes, onion and Easy Garlic in a baking dish, drizzle with extra virgin olive oil and season with oregano, chilli flakes and black pepper.
Nestle the block of feta into the centre, arrange the chicken around it and turn the chicken in the seasoned oil.
Bake until the tomatoes are soft, the feta is melting and the chicken is cooked through with no pink remaining.
Cook the wholemeal penne until al dente while the chicken and tomatoes roast, reserving a little pasta water.
Mash the feta and tomatoes together, then stir in enough pasta water to make a silky sauce.
Wilt the baby spinach into the sauce, then toss through the penne, cooked chicken and fresh basil.
Check the seasoning and serve immediately.
tip: Cut the chicken into evenly sized pieces so it cooks through at the same time as the tomatoes and feta.

name: Chicken, Spinach & Chickpea Curry
description: With Wholegrain Brown Rice & Fresh Coriander
steps: Cook the wholegrain brown rice according to the packet instructions and keep it covered while you make the curry.
Cut the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces, then heat the extra virgin olive oil in a large pan.
Brown the chicken on all sides, add the onion and cook until softened.
Add the Easy Garlic, turmeric, cumin, ground coriander, ground ginger and chilli flakes, then stir for one minute.
Stir in the chickpeas and chopped tomatoes and bring the sauce to a gentle simmer.
Cook until the sauce thickens and the chicken is cooked through with no pink remaining.
Add the baby spinach, let it wilt, then season and stir through most of the fresh coriander.
Serve the curry over the rice with the remaining coriander on top.
tip: Microwave wholegrain rice saves a saucepan; keep the curry at a gentle simmer so the chicken stays tender.

name: Smoky Chicken, Butter Bean & Aubergine Stew
description: With Chorizo, Red Pepper & Rosemary
steps: Cut the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces and finely dice the chorizo.
Heat the extra virgin olive oil in a large casserole pan and cook the chorizo briefly until it releases its smoky oils.
Add the chicken and brown it lightly on all sides.
Add the fresh onion, red pepper and diced aubergine, then cook for 6–8 minutes until softened and lightly coloured.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, smoked paprika, chopped rosemary and black pepper for one minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes and drained butter beans, then bring to a gentle simmer.
Cook uncovered until the sauce has thickened and the chicken is cooked through with no pink remaining.
Stir through the red wine vinegar, check the seasoning without automatically adding salt, then serve in warm bowls.
tip: Dice the chorizo very finely so 40 g flavours the entire stew; it already supplies salt, so taste before adding any more.

name: Turkey & Red Lentil Bolognese
description: With Wholemeal Penne, Spinach & Fresh Basil
steps: Rinse the red lentils under cold water and leave them to drain.
Heat the extra virgin olive oil in a large pan and soften the fresh onion.
Add the turkey mince and cook until lightly browned, breaking it up with a spoon.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, dried oregano and black pepper for one minute.
Add the passata and red lentils with enough water to keep the sauce loose, then simmer until the lentils are tender.
Cook the wholemeal penne until al dente while the sauce simmers, reserving a little pasta water.
Wilt the baby spinach into the bolognese and loosen with pasta water if needed.
Toss with the penne, stir through the fresh basil and serve.
tip: The lentils thicken as they cook, so add water gradually rather than letting the sauce catch on the bottom of the pan.

name: BBQ Turkey Breast with Butter Bean Salad
description: With Roasted Peppers, Cherry Tomatoes & Parsley
steps: Coat the turkey breast with one tablespoon of extra virgin olive oil, Easy Garlic, smoked paprika, oregano and black pepper.
Heat the BBQ for direct cooking and allow the turkey to marinate while it comes to temperature.
Drain and rinse the butter beans, then slice the roasted red peppers, cherry tomatoes and red onion.
Combine the beans and vegetables in a large serving bowl.
Whisk the lemon juice and zest with the remaining olive oil and chopped parsley, then fold through the salad.
Cook the turkey on the BBQ until browned and cooked through with no pink remaining.
Rest the turkey for five minutes before slicing it across the grain.
Serve the sliced turkey over the butter bean salad with any remaining lemon dressing.
tip: Flatten thicker parts of the turkey breast to an even depth so the meat cooks at the same rate and stays juicy.

name: BBQ Chicken Caprese Skewers
description: With Pesto Quinoa, Mozzarella & Fresh Basil
steps: If using wooden skewers, soak them in water while the BBQ heats for direct cooking.
Cut the chicken, courgettes and red peppers into even bite-sized pieces.
Thread the chicken, vegetables and cherry tomatoes onto skewers, then brush with half the pesto mixed with Easy Garlic and black pepper.
Cook the skewers on the BBQ, turning regularly, until the vegetables are charred and the chicken is cooked through.
Cook the quinoa according to the packet instructions, then fluff it with a fork.
Stir the remaining pesto, lemon juice, lemon zest and most of the fresh basil through the quinoa.
Rest the skewers briefly, then serve them over the pesto quinoa.
Tear over the mozzarella and finish with the remaining basil.
tip: Keep the chicken and vegetables similar in size and leave a little space between pieces so the BBQ heat can circulate.

name: Healthy Chicken, Chorizo & Seafood Paella
description: With Prawns, Mussels & Peas
steps: Warm the low-salt chicken stock and stir in the turmeric while you prepare the other ingredients.
Heat the extra virgin olive oil in a wide paella pan, cook the finely diced chorizo briefly, then add and lightly brown the chicken.
Add the fresh onion and red pepper and cook until softened.
Stir in the Easy Garlic, smoked paprika, black pepper and paella rice until every grain is coated.
Add the chopped tomatoes and seasoned stock, spread the rice evenly and simmer gently without stirring until most of the liquid is absorbed.
Nestle the cleaned mussels into the rice, cover loosely and cook until they begin to open.
Add the prawns and frozen peas and cook until the prawns are opaque, the mussels are open and the chicken is cooked through; discard any mussels that remain closed.
Rest for five minutes, then finish with lemon juice, lemon wedges and chopped parsley.
tip: Use the widest pan you have and resist stirring after adding the stock; the thin rice layer cooks more evenly and can develop a lightly toasted base.
